# GitHub Layout, Compact Calendar, and Titlebar Clock Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Correct the GitHub page control placement, replace the oversized contribution grid with a compact month calendar, and move the live clock into the application titlebar.

**Architecture:** Keep the existing renderer-owned GitHub data and refresh flow, changing only the HTML generated by `app.js` and the corresponding CSS. Extend the current renderer contract tests so structural behavior is locked before styling, then verify the complete Electron workspace.

**Tech Stack:** Electron 40, vanilla JavaScript, HTML, CSS, Node.js built-in test runner.

## Global Constraints

- Preserve existing GitHub refresh, profile opening, month navigation, tooltip, and keyboard behavior.
- Keep `#system-clock` as the live clock hook while moving it out of page content.
- Do not modify GitHub API or backend data contracts.
- Preserve unrelated working-tree changes.

---

### Task 1: Lock the revised renderer structure with tests

**Files:**
- Modify: `apps/windows-electron/src/renderer/security.test.js`
- Modify: `apps/windows-electron/src/renderer/app.js`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: existing `renderGithubDashboard()` markup, `#refresh-github`, `#system-clock`, and `[data-month-direction]` handlers.
- Produces: `.github-heading-actions`, `.github-profile-status`, `.github-profile-open`, `.github-calendar-title`, and `[data-month-today]` markup hooks.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write failing contract assertions**

Add source-level assertions that the renderer contains the five new hooks, renders `#system-clock` inside `.titlebar-clock`, and no longer places the clock in `.content-header`.

- [ ] **Step 2: Run the focused test and confirm failure**

Run: `node --test apps/windows-electron/src/renderer/security.test.js`

Expected: FAIL because the new structural hooks and titlebar clock placement do not exist.

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement the structural markup**

Update `renderGithubDashboard()` so the refresh button is inside `.github-heading-actions`; split profile actions into `.github-profile-status` and `.github-profile-open`; render month abbreviation, year, previous, `TODAY`, and next controls in `.github-calendar-title`; render the clock in `.titlebar-clock` next to the window controls and remove the content-header clock.

- [ ] **Step 4: Add the TODAY interaction**

In the renderer click delegation, handle `[data-month-today]` by setting `githubMonthIndex` to the current/latest month index and rerendering the GitHub page, without altering `[data-month-direction]` behavior.

- [ ] **Step 5: Run the focused test**

Run: `node --test apps/windows-electron/src/renderer/security.test.js`

Expected: PASS.

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit the structural change**

Run: `git add apps/windows-electron/src/renderer/app.js apps/windows-electron/src/renderer/security.test.js && git commit -m "feat: restructure GitHub activity controls"`

### Task 2: Apply compact calendar and collision-free layout styles

**Files:**
- Modify: `apps/windows-electron/src/renderer/styles.css`
- Test: `apps/windows-electron/src/renderer/security.test.js`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: markup hooks introduced by Task 1.
- Produces: responsive title/header, profile action rail, compact date grid, compact statistics strip, and titlebar clock presentation.

- [ ] **Step 1: Add failing CSS contract assertions**

Assert the stylesheet includes `.titlebar-clock`, `.github-heading-actions`, `.github-profile-status`, `.github-profile-open`, `.github-calendar-title`, and a compact `.github-calendar-grid` row definition without the prior `clamp(28px, 5.5vw, 36px)` tile sizing.

- [ ] **Step 2: Run the focused test and confirm failure**

Run: `node --test apps/windows-electron/src/renderer/security.test.js`

Expected: FAIL on missing compact-layout CSS contracts.

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement desktop styles**

Make `.github-page-heading` a two-column title/action row; give the profile action rail separate top and bottom alignment; style the calendar title after the supplied reference; make date cells borderless and compact; keep contribution levels visible through text/background emphasis; reduce the statistic strip padding and type sizes; style `.titlebar-clock` immediately left of window controls with `-webkit-app-region: no-drag`.

- [ ] **Step 4: Implement responsive styles**

At existing renderer breakpoints, allow the heading to wrap, convert profile actions to a full-width two-column row, reduce calendar gaps and type sizes, and hide the clock date before it could collide with window controls.

- [ ] **Step 5: Run focused and syntax tests**

Run: `node --test apps/windows-electron/src/renderer/security.test.js`

Run: `npm run check:syntax --workspace @winplate/windows-electron`

Expected: both commands PASS.

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit the visual change**

Run: `git add apps/windows-electron/src/renderer/styles.css apps/windows-electron/src/renderer/security.test.js && git commit -m "style: compact GitHub calendar and titlebar clock"`

### Task 3: Verify behavior and rendered appearance

**Files:**
- Modify only if verification reveals a scoped defect: `apps/windows-electron/src/renderer/app.js`, `apps/windows-electron/src/renderer/styles.css`, `apps/windows-electron/src/renderer/security.test.js`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: completed renderer markup and CSS.
- Produces: verified app behavior and a screenshot showing the corrected layout.

- [ ] **Step 1: Run the complete project check**

Run: `npm run check`

Expected: all syntax, unit, layout, Python-service, and workspace tests PASS.

- [ ] **Step 2: Launch the Electron app**

Run: `npm run dev`

Expected: WinPlate opens without renderer errors.

- [ ] **Step 3: Perform visual and interaction QA**

Open GitHub and verify: refresh aligns with the title; Live and Open do not overlap; the month calendar matches the compact reference density; the clock appears only in the titlebar; previous, TODAY, next, refresh, and Open GitHub actions work; common narrow and wide sizes do not clip.

- [ ] **Step 4: Capture a final screenshot**

Save the verified GitHub page screenshot under `docs/qa/` with a descriptive `2026-07-11-github-compact-calendar-current.png` filename.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit any verification artifact or scoped correction**

Run: `git add docs/qa/2026-07-11-github-compact-calendar-current.png apps/windows-electron/src/renderer/app.js apps/windows-electron/src/renderer/styles.css apps/windows-electron/src/renderer/security.test.js && git commit -m "test: verify compact GitHub activity layout"`
